reason: Both answers reach the same well-reasoned recommendation (take the deal conditional on having a clear use of funds that accelerates growth), correctly frame default-alive status as giving leverage rather than survival pressure, compute the ~17% dilution correctly, and note 8%/mo is solid-not-breakout. Both raise the venture-path tradeoff (bootstrapped profitable lifestyle business vs. VC growth trajectory) and both note the leverage to negotiate. The differentiator is that Answer A explicitly covers deal terms beyond headline valuation (1x non-participating preference, avoiding aggressive board control at seed) — one of the key points the rubric calls out — while Answer B only discusses negotiating the price/valuation and never mentions preference terms or board seat. This makes A marginally more complete without being longer in a padded way. The two are otherwise comparable in clarity and concision.
